feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: After new drug’s ‘unprecedented’ results for pancreatic cancer, doctors look at other uses
entry.summary: The experimental drug daraxonrasib, which doubled survival time in patients with advanced pancreatic cancer, may also prove effective for lung, colon and ovarian cancers.

entry.title: Startup Testing Drugs on Freshly Extracted Human Brains That Are Kept On Life Support
entry.summary: The biotech startup Bexorg keeps the brains running for 24 hours before they're sliced apart and examined by scientists.

entry.title: How Nigeria’s ‘algorithmic apothecary’ fuels a surge in risky herbal cures
entry.summary: Unverified herbal remedies promoted on social media are driving rising health risks and delayed treatment in Nigeria.

entry.title: 4 High-Fat Foods You Should Be Eating to Lower High Blood Pressure, According to Experts
entry.summary: Supporting healthy blood pressure doesn’t mean you need to cut all fat from your diet. Learn about four fatty foods that can benefit heart health and blood pressure.

entry.title: Giant Study Reveals The Secret to Heart Health Isn't Low-Carb or Low-Fat
entry.summary: The key to heart health isn't cutting down on pasta or potatoes, new evidence suggests; it's not even a low-fat diet.

entry.title: Do you need SPF 30, 50 or 100? Dermatologists answer your burning sunscreen questions
entry.summary: PolitiFact asked board-certified dermatologists to answer people's burning questions about SPF. When it comes to sunscreen, they advised buying broad-spectrum sunscreen that’s SPF 30 or higher and reapplying regularly.

entry.title: For Gen Z, 'Tanmaxxing' Rules, Despite Skin Cancer Warnings
entry.summary: Myths proliferating on social media, low cancer fears are fueling risky tanning trends

entry.title: Rabid bat found in residential area triggers warning from police
entry.summary: A rabid bat found near a Bay Area residential neighborhood has sparked a warning from the police about the dangerous exposure to humans and pets.

entry.title: The unpredictable disease attacking women 5 times more than men — doctors only study it for ‘one minute’
entry.summary: The condition is elusive and shifts without warning. Patients eat normally one day, then go into anaphylaxis the next. Reactions can be triggered by stress,...

entry.title: Man Can't Work Because He Gets Drunk Without Drinking
entry.summary: Rare gut condition leaves Nova Scotia father frequently, unpredictably intoxicated

entry.title: 18 Funny Women Who Made May Worth Every Aggravating Moment
entry.summary: "Can my boyfriend come?' Can my flesh-eating virus come?"

entry.title: Former Pensacola Police officer arrested on 2 new charges of HIV nondisclosure during sex
entry.summary: PENSACOLA, Fla. -- A now-former Pensacola Police officer is facing two new charges of failing to disclose his HIV-positive status to a sexual partner. This foll

entry.title: Dispute grows over home monitoring of hantavirus passengers
entry.summary: White House officials have been pushing for aggressive monitoring at home for those who return from the quarantine facility, but has retreated from initial proposals. Negotiations are ongoing with state officials.

entry.title: WHO chief visits Ebola outbreak epicenter in eastern Congo as cases outpace response
entry.summary: The health organization said latest official figures showed 906 suspected cases and 223 suspected deaths.

entry.title: The Most Satisfying Snack for Weight Loss (Hint: It’s Not Almonds)
entry.summary: There's a reason dietitians reach for this protein-packed snack over a handful of almonds.

entry.title: What Scientists Found Inside a 117-Year-Old Woman Reveals New Clues to Long Life
entry.summary: A remarkable new study of the world’s oldest verified living person reveals a surprising picture of extreme longevity.

entry.title: Jacksonville girl heading to NYC for cancer treatment after bone cancer returns; Community raising money to cover costs
entry.summary: What happens next is Gianna and her family will be heading to New York City where Gianna has been accepted to be a part of an experimental trial for the next phase or her cancer treatment. That is going to be at Memorial Sloan Kettering (MSK) Cancer Center in…

entry.title: Fox Chapel woman’s battle with melanoma included immunotherapy
entry.summary: Christy Redican grew up in Connecticut, spending summers sailing and playing tennis. “I was a sun worshipper,” she said. “I did all the things you’re not supposed to do. I have fair skin, blonde hair and light eyes. I burned a lot.&rdq…

entry.title: Doctors reveal healthiest fast-food meals and the menu items they say to avoid
entry.summary: Doctors weigh in on the most nutritious fast-food meals, warning that processed ingredients and low protein can make seemingly healthy options misleading.

entry.title: WHO Chief Visits Bunia—Epicenter Of Africa’s Ebola Outbreak (Live Updates)
entry.summary: There have been more than 220 suspected deaths since the start of the Ebola outbreak.

entry.title: More than half of adults with ADHD in clinical settings have a co-occurring personality disorder
entry.summary: A new analysis suggests that 57 percent of adults seeking help for ADHD also meet the criteria for a personality disorder. Researchers found that conditions like avoidant and borderline personality are surprisingly common in this population.

entry.title: As Summer Nears, 'Winter Vomiting Disease' Surges
entry.summary: Wastewater data, CDC stats show elevated norovirus infections nationwide

entry.title: Allergy seasons merge on Long Island causing 'double whammy'
entry.summary: "With particularly cold winters and an early spring, everything sort of pops at once," said one allergy specialist.

entry.title: Cats could help scientists better understand human cancer, study says
entry.summary: A study of nearly 500 domestic cats uncovered similarities between cancer gene mutations in felines and humans.

entry.title: Inside Alzheimer's neurons, tau may set off a genetic chain reaction that ends in cell death
entry.summary: Alzheimer's disease is a neurodegenerative disease characterized by a progressive decline in mental functions and memory loss. Along with frontotemporal dementia and some other neurodegenerative disorders, Alzheimer's disease has been associated with an accum…

entry.title: The $950 cancer blood test is here: 5 things to consider before ordering yours
entry.summary: Grail's multi-cancer blood test can find cancer — but there's still one big problem with how it works.

entry.title: Here's What It Means If You Get Winded From Walking Up The Stairs
entry.summary: Most people have been out of breath after climbing the stairs — but is that normal?

entry.title: Inside Europe's largest Copper Age tomb, children's bones expose an ancient health crisis hidden for 5,000 years
entry.summary: Nearly 5,000 years ago, respiratory infections, possibly including tuberculosis, were ravaging the children buried at Camino del Molino (CMOL), Spain. The massive circular burial cave carved into rock is Europe's largest Copper Age mass burial, containing ove…

entry.title: Military Spouse, 38, Diagnosed With Colon Cancer, Doctors Dismissed It
entry.summary: A woman was experiencing a common symptom of colon cancer for years, but doctors dismissed it as pregnancy-related. She was diagnosed with stage 3c.

entry.title: A Texas woman thought her new diet was causing digestive issues. Doctors found cancer: "I couldn't believe it"
entry.summary: Candace Tucker thought her symptoms were benign. A colonoscopy led to an alarming diagnosis.

entry.title: 5 flesh-eating bacteria cases confirmed in US, more expected in summer
entry.summary: Five cases of flesh-eating bacteria were confirmed in a popular American tourist destination, and officials anticipate the number could rise in the summer.

entry.title: If You Sleep In This 1 Position, Doctors Say You Need To Stop Immediately
entry.summary: It may feel safe and comfy, but experts warn it could lead to lasting nerve damage.

entry.title: Drinking These Beloved Beverages May Be Linked With A Lower Dementia Risk, Study Finds
entry.summary: Researchers evaluated participants for over 40 years and found interesting takeaways related to their dietary intake and cognitive health.

entry.title: Ebola response puts Trump on collision course with global health body
entry.summary: The Trump administration has implemented some of the United States’ most stringent travel restrictions for infectious disease ever.

entry.title: 64 People Reveal Their Fascinating "Medical Mysteries" They Didn't Know Were "Uncommon" At All
entry.summary: "Apparently, some people have an 'internal monologue'?"

entry.title: Google Seeks Federal Approval to Release 32 Million Treated Mosquitoes in California
entry.summary: California stands at the center of a novel public health experiment that could reshape how mosquito-borne illnesses are managed in the United States. Google ...

entry.title: Vibrio vulnificus has returned to Florida beaches
entry.summary: Florida has logged more Vibrio cases early in 2026 as the high-risk summer season approaches.

entry.title: Eating ice cream too fast? Brain freeze says a surprising amount about your health
entry.summary: If you’re having ice cream headaches often, it might be worth seeing if it runs in your family and taking a second look at your non-brain-freeze headaches too.

entry.title: Millions of breast cancer patients could safely avoid chemotherapy, study suggests
entry.summary: A new DNA test could help screen whether patients require the treatment or not, according to a new international trial.

entry.title: Nutritionist explains how eating late at night may be affecting your hormones, sleep, and weight | Health
entry.summary: Late-night eating habits are seen as the new normal, but they could be slowing your metabolism and increasing belly fat. | Health

entry.title: Mpox cases in Boston on the rise despite declining nationally
entry.summary: Between August 1, 2025, and March 31, 2026, 77 mpox cases were reported in Massachusetts, according to officials.

entry.title: What do common dementia risk factors have in common? Poor sleep
entry.summary: Different chronic conditions that are also risk factors for dementia appear to be linked to the disruption of a sleep-dependent brain rhythm that helps clear 'waste' from the brain, a new review highlights.

entry.title: Showing my hernia makes me less self-conscious
entry.summary: Amy Bamford has taken to social media to talk about her "massive" incisional hernia.

entry.title: Google planning to release millions of mosquitoes into California to help stop diseases
entry.summary: California could soon become a testing ground for one of Google’s most ambitious public health projects yet. The tech giant is seeking federal approval to release up to 32 million specially t…

entry.title: Former CDC director believes US will likely see Ebola cases
entry.summary: A new outbreak of Ebola, caused by the Bundibugyo virus, has spread undetected for weeks across Congo and into Uganda.

entry.title: Experts Say This Nighttime Habit Is Linked with Poor Liver Health
entry.summary: Experts share the top nighttime habit that may be harming liver health, along with other tips for protecting your liver.

entry.title: VDH reports measles case at St. Mary’s Hospital
entry.summary: The Virginia Department of Health is reporting a new measles exposure in Richmond.

entry.title: How learning to read alters the brain’s approach to spoken language
entry.summary: Learning to read does more than help you understand written text. A new brain imaging study reveals that formal reading education physically changes the neurological pathways humans use to process and decode spoken language.

entry.title: Two North Texas counties monitoring travelers for Ebola symptoms
entry.summary: Tarrant and Denton counties are checking in with travelers for three weeks after they return from Ebola‑affected countries.

entry.title: It’s Alive? Surprising Discovery Changes What We Know About Fog
entry.summary: Scientists discovered that fog droplets can host living bacteria that grow and help remove harmful pollutants from the atmosphere, revealing fog as a surprisingly active microbial environment.
